title: arrhenatherum elatius subsp. sardoum
intro: Arrhenatherum elatius subsp. sardoum, also known as Sardinian oat-grass or Arrhenatherum sardoum, is a subspecies of the perennial grass Arrhenatherum elatius, native to the island of Sardinia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. Arrhenatherum elatius subsp. sardoum can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
